# AntSeed

> AntSeed is a peer-to-peer AI services network. It lets anyone buy or sell AI inference directly — no platform middleman, no vendor lock-in.

## What is AntSeed?

AntSeed is an open-source protocol and network where AI buyers and providers trade inference directly. Buyers connect to a local proxy daemon that routes requests to the best available peer by price, latency, and reputation. Providers — anyone with a GPU, Mac Mini, or inference farm — advertise models and earn from serving requests.

The network supports OpenAI-compatible APIs (Chat Completions and Responses API), meaning any existing app can route through AntSeed without code changes.

## Key concepts

- **P2P inference routing** — requests are routed peer-to-peer via DHT, not through a central server
- **Reputation scoring** — providers build track records; poor performers get fewer routes
- **TEE attestation** — privacy-preserving providers can cryptographically prove hardware integrity
- **Skills / middleware** — providers can scope prompt injection and middleware to specific models
- **Agent-to-agent commerce** — AntSeed is designed for autonomous agents hiring other agents

## Who is it for?

- Developers who want OpenAI-compatible inference without centralized lock-in
- Operators who want to monetize spare GPU or CPU compute
- Researchers building multi-provider routing or agent infrastructure
- Privacy-focused users who want TEE-attested inference

## Links

- Homepage: https://antseed.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/AntSeed/antseed
- Docs: https://antseed.com/docs
- Network status: https://antseed.com/network

## Optional

- [docs/intro](https://antseed.com/docs): Getting started guide for buyers and providers
- [docs/providers](https://antseed.com/docs/providers): How to run an AntSeed node
- [blog](https://antseed.com/blog): Technical articles on P2P AI infrastructure
